nodes.label: WHAT HAPPENS WHEN AI HITS ITS LIMITS
YOUR FILESYSTEM IS YOUR ORG CHART
CASE_DRAFTED
nodes.emitted_by: Internal — emitted when Anthropic tool_use `record_case_draft(case_yaml)` fires from the CaseChat agent and the worker writes the DRAFT row to D1 `casechat_cases`. Tool-use is the SOLE governance path per TALKS/CASECHAT/INTEL.md (Wave 1 HTML-comment harvest dropped 2026-05-06 — fail fast, no fallbacks). Idempotent on `(author_id, phenotype_hash)` within 60s window.

nodes.label: CASE_REVIEW_REQUESTED
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/casechat/case/:id/review` (author submits DRAFT for IN_REVIEW; routes to faculty queue)

nodes.label: CASE_PEER_REVIEW_SIGNED
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/casechat/case/:id/review/sign` (single faculty reviewer attests case quality + de-identification + clinical interpretation)

nodes.label: CASE_PUBLISHED
nodes.emitted_by: Internal — emitted when 2nd CASE_PEER_REVIEW_SIGNED with decision=accept arrives, locks state

nodes.label: CASE_RETRACTED
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/casechat/case/:id/retract` (rare — e.g., de-id breach discovered post-publication; marker only, original record preserved per APPEND_ONLY)

nodes.label: CASE_EXPORTED
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/casechat/case/:id/export` (clinician exports a PUBLISHED case to PDF for grand-rounds / EMR / teaching; ledgered for usage analytics + author-attribution surfaces)

nodes.label: CASE_SIMULATED
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/casechat/case/sim` (CaseChat operator authors a simulated exemplar — `simulated: true`, never confused with PUBLISHED real cases per TALKS/CASECHAT/INTEL.md § Exemplar Cases; ledgered for schema-coverage analytics + chat-prompt-corpus attribution)

nodes.label: MANUSCRIPT_BODY_EDITED
nodes.emitted_by: Internal — emitted when Anthropic tool_use `record_case_draft` re-fires for an existing case_id (subsequent draft turns refining the same SIM- or published case) and the worker applies a field-level update to D1 `casechat_cases`. Distinct from CASE_DRAFTED which is the first-write-of-record event. Per TALKS/CASECHAT/INTEL.md § Tool side-effect contract.

nodes.label: CASECHAT_VALIDATION_RECORDED
nodes.emitted_by: Internal — emitted when Anthropic tool_use `record_validation(target_ref, decision, replacement, comment)` fires from the CaseChat agent in Validate Mode and the worker writes a row to D1 `casechat_validations`. Decision ∈ VALIDATE/REFUTE/UPDATE/EXTEND. Tool-use is the SOLE governance path per TALKS/CASECHAT/INTEL.md (Wave 1 HTML-comment harvest dropped 2026-05-06).

nodes.label: CASECHAT_SESSION_STARTED
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/talk/casechat/threads` — emitted when a CaseChat thread is created, immediately after SESSION_LEDGERED. Fires for every CaseChat session regardless of whether a case is drafted. Provides CaseChat-scoped session visibility — without this, a CaseChat session where the clinician explores but does not call record_case_draft emits zero CASECHAT-family events. One row per thread creation.

nodes.label: TOOL_USE_REJECTED
nodes.emitted_by: Internal — emitted when a tool_use call from the CaseChat agent fails fast: bad args (input_schema validation failure), missing or unrecognized author credential (must be FACMG/MD/PhD/board_certified per VITAE), or scope mismatch. Pairs with HTTP 400/403 to the client. Audit surface for governance drift; never silently swallowed.

nodes.label: TOOL_USE_FAILED
nodes.emitted_by: Internal — emitted when a tool_use call validates but the downstream write fails (D1 transient error, KV write timeout, anthropic API edge case). Distinct from TOOL_USE_REJECTED which is governance-rejection. Pairs with HTTP 502 + retryable client message.

nodes.label: APP_DEPLOY_STARTED
nodes.emitted_by: bin/deploy-apps at lockfile acquisition for a pages_project — per SERVICES/BUILD/CANON § Deploy Concurrency Contract. Pair {STARTED, COMPLETED\

nodes.label: APP_DEPLOY_COMPLETED
nodes.emitted_by: bin/deploy-apps at successful exit of a monorepo deploy (next-on-pages build + wrangler pages deploy + hostname reconcile all OK).

nodes.label: APP_DEPLOY_FAILED
nodes.emitted_by: bin/deploy-apps at failure exit of a monorepo deploy — signal, exception, build error, or wrangler-deploy error. Pairs with APP_DEPLOY_STARTED so orphan-lock detector can find unpaired starts.

nodes.label: DEPLOY_LOCK_RECOVERED
nodes.emitted_by: bin/deploy-apps at startup when stale lockfile (dead PID) is removed before proceeding. Distinct from STARTED to surface lock recovery in audit.

nodes.label: AXIOM_FRESHENED
nodes.emitted_by: Phase 09j — `build-axiom-freshen` on deterministic restructure of a legacy `## Axiom` section. One row per CANON.md touched. Shares the BACKPROP ledger dir + hash-chain (per SERVICES/LEDGER/CANON.md Chain invariant) — `magic_lib.ledger.emit_backprop_event()` is the single emitter

nodes.label: AXIOM_FRESHEN_PROPOSED
nodes.emitted_by: Phase 09j — `build-axiom-freshen` when deterministic restructure cannot produce a structured section passing magic-validate. Writes `SERVICES/BACKPROP/PROPOSED/axiom-freshen-<hash>.md` for the operator (human or agent-in-conversation with full repo context) to restructure by hand; next build's ratify sweep applies the fix

nodes.label: AXIOM_FRESHEN_SCAFFOLDED
nodes.emitted_by: Phase 09j — `build-axiom-freshen` when a child scope inherits axioms from its parent (child had no Axiom section at all). Matches the scaffold behavior governed in SERVICES/BACKPROP/CANON § axiom inheritance

nodes.label: AXIOM_FRESHEN_RATIFIED
nodes.emitted_by: Phase 09j — `build-axiom-freshen` ratify sweep: a previously PROPOSED axiom-freshen target is now structured (human or subsequent deterministic run closed the loop). Moves the PROPOSED file to APPLIED/

nodes.label: AXIOM_FRESHEN_ORPHANED
nodes.emitted_by: Phase 09j — `build-axiom-freshen` ratify sweep: a PROPOSED axiom-freshen target's legacy `## Axiom` section was removed or the file no longer exists. Moves the PROPOSED file to REJECTED/

nodes.label: CANON_SECTION_STRIPPED
nodes.emitted_by: Phase 09 — `build-canon-strip` (lib.freshen.canon) on idempotent removal of a redundant section (`## Purpose`, `## Overview`, etc.) or `## Axiom` coda from a `CANON.md`. One row per file touched, payload lists the section names removed. Reversible via git revert.

nodes.label: CROSS_PROPAGATED
nodes.emitted_by: Phase 09 — `cross-propagate` (lib.freshen.cross_propagate) on axiom-relevant pattern propagation across CROSS_INTEL edges. One row per source pattern × target scope pair. Idempotent via `learning/cross-propagated.json` state file (per-source `propagated_at` + `targets` count). Reversible via git revert.

nodes.label: LEDGER_PROJECTED
nodes.emitted_by: Phase 09 — `ledger-sync` (lib.freshen.ledger) on idempotent projection of a KV ledger entry onto its scope's `LEARNING.md`. Reads `/talk/ledger?since=<ts>` via api.canonic.org (one bulk call), groups by scope, dedups by row-hash, appends new rows. Idempotent via per-scope `last_ts` in `LEARNING/ledger-sync.json`. One ledger row per scope batch, payload includes the count + newest_ts. Reversible via git revert.

nodes.label: ADMIN_SCOPE_CREATED
nodes.emitted_by: Phase 09 — `reconcile-admin-mutations` (lib.freshen.admin_mutations) on draining a `admin:pending:scope:*` KV mutation into the GOV filesystem. Creates the scope dir + initial files (CANON.md / INTEL.md / etc.) under the parent. KV key deleted on success.

nodes.label: ADMIN_INTEL_UPDATED
nodes.emitted_by: Phase 09 — `reconcile-admin-mutations` on draining a `admin:pending:intel:*` KV mutation. Appends or creates `INTEL.md` for the target scope. KV key deleted on success.

nodes.label: ADMIN_LEARNING_ADDED
nodes.emitted_by: Phase 09 — `reconcile-admin-mutations` on draining a `admin:pending:learning:*` KV mutation. Appends one row to the target scope's `LEARNING.md` (creates the file with header if missing). KV key deleted on success.

nodes.label: LEARNING_EDIT

nodes.label: MANUSCRIPT_EDIT

nodes.label: MANUSCRIPT_COAUTHOR_INVITED
nodes.emitted_by: `magic_lib.coauthor.invite()` — operator-initiated; system drafts magic-link mail into `~/Library/MAIL/drafts/`. Owning service: SERVICES/COAUTHOR. Per CANON axiom CONSENT_LEDGER_BACKED.

nodes.label: PRINCIPAL_GITHUB_INVITED
nodes.emitted_by: Operator-initiated GitHub-collaborator invitation for a CANONIC governance principal — emitted by `lib.ledger.emit_principal_event(LEDGER_TYPES.PRINCIPAL_GITHUB_INVITED, ...)` when a `USERS/<NAME>` principal's `github_login` is invited as a collaborator on a hadleylab-canonic / canonic-canonic / .canonic repository (pull/triage/push/maintain/admin permission). Owning service: SERVICES/USER (binds GitHub login to principal identity per VITAE registry; closes the file-state-as-ledger gap surfaced 2026-05-07 during SIMO intake — Beriba-cloud invites #317859191 + #317859208 were captured only in VITAE Relationship block until this event type landed). First member of the PRINCIPAL_* family; future siblings reserved: `PRINCIPAL_GITHUB_ACCEPTED` (webhook on invite acceptance), `PRINCIPAL_GITHUB_REVOKED` (collaborator removal).

nodes.label: CASENEX_CASE_UTILITY_SIGNAL
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/casenex/signal/utility` — reader signals a PUBLISHED case was clinically useful. Parallel to ANKI_DIFFICULTY_SIGNAL. Aggregated at the case level; no per-reader telemetry (HIPAA). Accepts `?dry_run=1` — payload validation runs, D1 insert + ledger row + COIN mint skipped; response `{ok: true, dry_run: true, validated: true}`. Unauthenticated (CaseNex is read-open); `anon` rate-limit tier. case_id + scope validated against published case registry. Owning service: APPS/CASENEX.

nodes.label: CASENEX_CASE_DOWNLOADED
nodes.emitted_by: Edge event on case bundle (`.json` + `.pdf` pair) fetch from R2, emitted by `bin/build-casenex-bundles` edge handler. One row per download; client_hash rotates daily (no per-reader tracking). Owning service: APPS/CASENEX.

nodes.label: CASENEX_REFERRAL_INVITED
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/casenex/referral/invite-prepare` (authed) — reader prepares an outbound CaseNex referral invite. Outgoing invite telemetry; recipient email NEVER stored. Parallel to ANKI_REFERRAL_INVITED. Owning service: APPS/CASENEX.

nodes.label: CASENEX_REFERRAL_REGISTERED
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/auth/register` carrying a valid CaseNex `referral_code` — new reader registers via a CaseNex referral link. Dedup: 1-per-(referrer, registrant) pair. Parallel to ANKI_REFERRAL_REGISTERED. Owning service: APPS/CASENEX.

nodes.label: CASENEX_REFERRAL_QUALIFIED
nodes.emitted_by: First qualifying non-dry-run `/casenex/signal/utility` from a reader who registered via a CaseNex referral code. Proves retention beyond signup. Dedup: 1-per-(referrer, referred) pair. Parallel to ANKI_REFERRAL_QUALIFIED. Owning service: APPS/CASENEX.

nodes.label: CASENEX_BETA_WELCOME
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/auth/register` during CaseNex beta window (APPS/CASENEX/COVERAGE.md § BETA_WINDOW_ACTIVE gate open). Dedup: 1-per-user lifetime. Parallel to ANKI_BETA_WELCOME. Owning service: APPS/CASENEX.

nodes.label: LAUDE_GALAXY_PROVISIONED
nodes.emitted_by: `POST /webhooks/github/laude-provisioner` — emitted on first creation of a `<login>/canonic-galaxy` repo via the `canonic-laude-provisioner` GitHub App `installation.created` webhook. One row per galaxy; idempotent on re-install (re-install does not re-seed; row emits only once per (github_login, repo) creation). Seeding writes 6 files (CANON.md, magic-manifest.json, MAGIC/SERVICES/LAUDE/CANON.md, TRANSCRIPTS/.gitkeep, INTEL/.gitkeep, README.md) as 1 commit. Ed25519 public key embedded in magic-manifest.json; private key delivered one-time in the welcome PR body (never persisted server-side post-delivery). Owning service: SERVICES/MAGIC/LAUDE.

nodes.label: LAUDE_TRANSCRIPT_LANDED
nodes.emitted_by: `POST /talk/laude/threads/:id/close` — emitted on every session-close PR opened in `<login>/canonic-galaxy`. One row per session-close, even when zero axioms were extracted. Transcript lands in `TRANSCRIPTS/<YYYY-MM-DD>/<thread-slug>.md`; axiom delta appended to `INTEL/<YYYY-MM>/laude-extracted.md`. PR auto-merges when diff ⊆ TRANSCRIPTS/ ∪ INTEL/ (scope check enforced by `verify-laude-pr-scope-policy`); CANON.md edits route to human review.

nodes.label: LAUDE_AXIOM_EXTRACTED
nodes.emitted_by: Internal — emitted per axiom queued via the `propose_axiom` tool call during an active LAUDE session. One row per axiom; batch-emitted atomically with LAUDE_TRANSCRIPT_LANDED on session-close. Payload MUST carry source thread_id + message_id for provenance trace; `verify-laude-axiom-extraction-traced` enforces non-null trace fields. Axiom payload: id (kebab slug), axiom (one governing sentence), scope_path (relative to canonic-galaxy root), trace (thread_id + message_id + excerpt ≤80 chars). Owning service: SERVICES/MAGIC/LAUDE.

nodes.label: LAUDE_CANON_UPDATED
nodes.emitted_by: Internal — emitted when an `update_canon` tool-use PR is merged by the user in `<login>/canonic-galaxy`. Human review always required; this row never emits from auto-merge (enforced by PR scope policy gate). Pairs with LAUDE_TRANSCRIPT_LANDED on the same session where the axiom was proposed. Owning service: SERVICES/MAGIC/LAUDE.

nodes.label: LAUDE_GALAXY_FEDERATED
nodes.emitted_by: Internal — emitted when a user opts their galaxy into cross-galaxy discovery by setting `federation: open` in their `magic-manifest.json` via the LAUDE galaxy settings surface. One row per (github_login, transition) — emits on open→closed and closed→open transitions. `verify-laude-scope-share-opt-in` reads the manifest before any cross-galaxy PR; this row is the audit trail of the posture change. Owning service: SERVICES/MAGIC/LAUDE.

nodes.label: LAUDE_MESSAGES_PURGED
nodes.emitted_by: Scheduled — emitted by `laudeMessagePurge` (daily 04:00 UTC) after deleting `laude_messages` rows whose `landed_at` is older than 24h. Closes NO_ANTHROPIC_DATA_RETENTION TRANSITIONAL; LAUDE CANON § Constraints: "Hold no messages.content beyond 24h". One row per purge cycle regardless of row count (zero-count emits are audit evidence of cycle health). Owning service: SERVICES/MAGIC/LAUDE.

nodes.label: LAUDE_SCOPE_SHARED
nodes.emitted_by: Internal — emitted when a cross-galaxy PR opened by the `share_scope` tool is merged by the recipient user. One row per accepted cross-galaxy share. The sharing user earns COIN; the receiving user's galaxy gains the scope subtree. Emitter: `POST /talk/laude/galaxies/:login/share` handler on PR merge webhook, not on PR open (unmerged shares do not mint). Owning service: SERVICES/MAGIC/LAUDE.

nodes.label: LAUDE_SESSION_STARTED
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/talk/laude/threads` — emitted when a LAUDE thread is created, immediately after SESSION_LEDGERED. Fires for every LAUDE session regardless of whether any axiom is extracted or session-close PR is opened. Provides LAUDE-scoped session visibility in the ledger — without this, a LAUDE session where propose_axiom is never called and End Session is not clicked emits zero LAUDE-family events (only generic CHAT_TURN rows). One row per thread creation.

nodes.label: LAUDE_SKETCH_GENERATED
nodes.emitted_by: `POST /talk/laude/sketch` — emitted when the sketch endpoint successfully generates a scope tree proposal. One row per sketch call. Does not emit if the LLM call fails or if tier validation fails. Owning service: SERVICES/MAGIC/LAUDE.

nodes.label: MANUSCRIPT_COAUTHOR_AUTHENTICATED
nodes.emitted_by: `magic_lib.coauthor.redeem_link()` (magic-link click → ORCID OAuth handshake). Identity claim ledgered. Owning service: SERVICES/COAUTHOR.

nodes.label: MANUSCRIPT_COAUTHOR_CONSENT_SIGNED
nodes.emitted_by: `magic_lib.coauthor.sign_consent()` — explicit "I authorize submission to <venue>" attestation against a specific draft SHA. Mints COIN per SERVICES/COIN § Mint Table (peer-review-grade attribution work). Owning service: SERVICES/COAUTHOR.

nodes.label: MANUSCRIPT_COAUTHOR_CONSENT_REVOKED
nodes.emitted_by: `magic_lib.coauthor.revoke_consent()` — pre-submission revocation; rare. Owning service: SERVICES/COAUTHOR.

nodes.label: MANUSCRIPT_INSTITUTIONAL_CLEARANCE_REQUESTED
nodes.emitted_by: `magic_lib.coauthor.request_clearance()` — coauthor or operator initiates IRB / COI-office / tech-transfer / PR-review request. Owning service: SERVICES/COAUTHOR.

nodes.label: MANUSCRIPT_INSTITUTIONAL_CLEARANCE_RECEIVED
nodes.emitted_by: `magic_lib.coauthor.record_clearance()` — operator records institutional decision (clearance comes via institutional email; operator stages the artifact + decision). Owning service: SERVICES/COAUTHOR.

nodes.label: MANUSCRIPT_ROSTER_RECONCILED
nodes.emitted_by: `bin/verify-coauthor-roster-reconciled` — per-venue diff between projection's roster snapshot and canonical `byline_orcids:` modulo declared overrides. Captures medRxiv-3 vs journal-7 diff and Forghani late-add cleanly. Owning service: SERVICES/COAUTHOR.

nodes.label: MANUSCRIPT_COI_BLOCK
nodes.emitted_by: `bin/verify-coauthor-coi-clear` — failure event; restricted-entity match against `~/.canonic/CAOH/restricted_entities.jsonl`. ORCID + email both checked. Per CANON axiom COI_FAILS_CLOSED. Owning service: SERVICES/COAUTHOR.

nodes.label: MANUSCRIPT_COMMENT_POSTED
nodes.emitted_by: `magic_lib.coauthor.post_comment()` — coauthor anchors a comment to a paragraph or figure. Mints COIN (peer-review-grade work, micro). Owning service: SERVICES/COAUTHOR.

nodes.label: MANUSCRIPT_COMMENT_RESOLVED
nodes.emitted_by: `magic_lib.coauthor.resolve_comment()` — author or commenter closes the thread (accepted/rejected/deferred). Owning service: SERVICES/COAUTHOR.

nodes.label: BLOG_EDIT
nodes.emitted_by: `magic_lib.writer.apply_edit()` on any `BLOGS/<scope>/<slug>.md` post artifact (`content_kind: prose`). Owning service: SERVICES/BLOG.

nodes.label: BOOK_EDIT
nodes.emitted_by: `magic_lib.writer.apply_edit()` on any `BOOKS/<scope>/CHAPTERS/*.md` artifact (`content_kind: prose`). Owning service: SERVICES/BOOK.

nodes.label: DECK_EDIT

nodes.label: UPTIME_PROBE
nodes.emitted_by: TASK UPTIME (worker, hourly) — HTTP probe of every governed origin. One row per probe summarizing pass/fail + latency

nodes.label: BRANDS_AUDIT
nodes.emitted_by: TASK BRANDS (worker, every 15min) — apex-route + brand-marker audit of every hostname declared in `APPS/CANONIC/app/lib/hostname-routes.json`. One row per audit cycle with the issue count

nodes.label: ONCO_SMOKE_RUN
nodes.emitted_by: TASK E2E-ONCO-LIVE-SMOKE (worker, hourly) — per-brand governance canary. Hits `api.canonic.org/config/<scope>` for each onco brand (MAMMOCHAT, CARIBCHAT, OMICSCHAT) and asserts `governance_status === 'composed'`. One row per cycle

nodes.label: SSL_EXPIRY_PROBE
nodes.emitted_by: TASK SSL-EXPIRY-WATCH (worker, daily) — per-registrable-domain TLS cert expiry probe via crt.sh Certificate Transparency log. Restores the SSL_DAYS_LEFT pre-warning the bash UPTIME script gave (openssl-derived); not feasible from inside CF Workers without external SSL state, so we use the public CT log. One row per cycle

nodes.label: E2E_SMOKE_PROBE
nodes.emitted_by: TASK E2E_SMOKE (worker, hourly) — HTTP-layer smoke of the governed e2e-flows manifest. For each flow tagged HTTP-EXECUTABLE (no fixture required, no multi-step round-trip, no credentialed cookie path), probe the URL and assert actual status matches expected. Browser-required flows are PENDING (await build-browser-e2e). One row per cycle

nodes.label: OAUTH_REFRESH
nodes.emitted_by: Access-token re-mint via service-account JWT exchange in `mail.js getAccessToken()` — one row per cache miss (KV TTL expired OR cold start). Observability signal for quota drift (burst of refreshes ≠ steady-state)

nodes.label: ART_MINTED
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/gallery/mint` (COMMERCE fleet member; creator-auth). One row per edition instance. Composes with SHOP ART_PIECE schema + SERVICES/COMMERCE CREATOR_CREDITED invariant. First row in a piece's provenance chain; `prev_hash` anchors to the preceding global LEDGER entry (cross-chain linkage via the shared ledger) while the piece's own provenance derives from successive ART_* rows matching `piece_id`.

nodes.label: ART_LISTED
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/gallery/list` (owner-auth). One row per listing event; a re-list after unlist is a new row with a new `listing_id`. Workers MUST reject listings when `piece.current_owner_id != seller_id`.

nodes.label: ART_SOLD
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/gallery/buy` (buyer-auth, credits-burn). One row per sale. Paired atomically with COIN_MINT (seller) and, on secondary sales, ROYALTY_PAID + COIN_MINT (original creator). `is_primary=true` when `seller_id == original_artist_id` AND `edition_num` has never appeared in a prior ART_SOLD for `piece_id`.

nodes.label: ART_TRANSFERRED
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/gallery/transfer` (owner-auth, non-sale). Gifts, loans, exhibition placements, estate transfers. Payload carries `reason` discriminator. Paired with no COIN mint (no money changed hands).

nodes.label: ROYALTY_PAID
nodes.emitted_by: Emitted atomically with secondary ART_SOLD by the same worker request. One row per secondary sale; the corresponding COIN_MINT to the original creator fires in the same D1 transaction. Invariant: every ART_SOLD with `is_primary=false` has exactly one paired ROYALTY_PAID row.

nodes.label: EXHIBITION_OPENED
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/gallery/exhibition` (curator-auth). Declares an exhibition with a list of pieces, venue, dates. Pieces referenced MUST exist; the row does not change ownership (loans for exhibition use ART_TRANSFERRED with `reason=exhibition`).

nodes.label: EXHIBITION_CLOSED
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/gallery/exhibition/close` (curator-auth) OR automatic via scheduled TASK at `closes_at`. Ledgers sales_count + transfer_count aggregated over the exhibition window for audit.

nodes.label: MIGRATION_IMPORTED
nodes.emitted_by: Emitted by governance-authored migration scripts when importing content from a legacy external surface (Strikingly, Wayback, shopify, squarespace, manual archive). One row per imported asset. Used to produce a provenance trail for pieces whose canonical origin predates COMMERCE.

nodes.label: FRAMEWORK_INGESTED
nodes.emitted_by: Phase `build-framework-registry` (SERVICES/FRAMEWORK § Compiler Contract) — one row per (framework_code, version) on first ingest into SERVICES/FRAMEWORK tree. PDF already hashed + stored at SOURCES/{org}/{year}/{slug}.pdf before this row emits.

nodes.label: FRAMEWORK_VERSIONED
nodes.emitted_by: Phase `build-framework-registry` detecting supersession (CANON.md declares new version whose `supersedes:` points to a prior version already in REGISTRY). Prior version stays governed; this row records the delta for downstream consumers.

nodes.label: FRAMEWORK_COMPETENCY_ADDED
nodes.emitted_by: Phase `build-framework-registry` detecting new competency IDs in a COMPETENCIES.md versus the prior REGISTRY snapshot. One row per added ID per build.

nodes.label: FRAMEWORK_COMPETENCY_DEPRECATED
nodes.emitted_by: Phase `build-framework-registry` detecting a `deprecated: true` flag added to a previously-active competency in COMPETENCIES.md. The competency is preserved in REGISTRY with the deprecated marker — downstream references continue to resolve.

nodes.label: FRAMEWORK_CARD_SEEDED
nodes.emitted_by: Phase `build-framework-anki-seed` (SERVICES/FRAMEWORK § Compiler Contract β) — one row per framework competency emitted as a framework-seeded community_thread into canonic-db. Idempotent; dedup is on (framework_code, competency_id) pair.

nodes.label: CAS_WRITE
nodes.emitted_by: CAS writer step 7 (SERVICES/CAS/CAS.md § Pipeline) — append-only accounting of every content-addressable write into `~/.canonic/cas/{substore}/{shard}/{rest}.json`. One row per write call; when the dedup step short-circuits (`dedup_hit=true`) the row still emits so retention accounting sees every reference attempt. `substore` ∈ {`idf`, `boundaries`, `patterns`} per SERVICES/CAS/CANON § three substores; payload hash inputs line up with the deduped envelope that producers (intake-promote, transcript scanner, Phase MB writer) supply.

nodes.label: ASSET_UPLOADED
nodes.emitted_by: PUT `/assets/:key` — `assetsPut` in `workers/api.canonic.org/src/assets.js` emits one row per governed R2 upload. Closes SERVICES/API/COVERAGE.md § MUTATION_LEDGER for `assetsPut` (the 31st mutating dispatch). R2 `customMetadata.sha256` remains as auxiliary content-hash provenance; the LEDGER row is the authoritative mutation record per SERVICES/API/CANON § MUTATION_LEDGERED axiom. Supersedes the stale `ASSET_PUT` row (never wired to an emitter).

nodes.label: COMPUTE_NODE_UP
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/compute/heartbeat` first-sight per (node_id, operator_id) OR re-registration after a prior COMPUTE_NODE_DOWN for the same node — `fleet-registry.js` in `workers/api.canonic.org/src/` (SERVICES/MAGIC/COMPUTE § Operator Node Contract). One row per node lifecycle transition into the eligible registry; subsequent heartbeats for the same live node do NOT emit. Attestation (App Attest assertion) verified against Apple CA before emission; unattested nodes are refused with HTTP 403 and never appear here.

nodes.label: COMPUTE_NODE_DOWN
nodes.emitted_by: 180-second heartbeat-gap sweep in `fleet-registry.js` OR explicit graceful shutdown POST `/compute/node/down` from agent on SIGTERM after in-flight drain. One row per node lifecycle transition out of the eligible registry. Coordinator MUST NOT route requests to a node after emission; in-flight requests at gap-time surface as failover via `fleet-failover.js`.

nodes.label: COMPUTE_SERVE
nodes.emitted_by: Emitted atomically on successful inference completion by `fleet.js::fleetComplete` / `fleetStream` in `workers/api.canonic.org/src/` (SERVICES/MAGIC/COMPUTE § Operator Node Contract). One row per served request, paired on (node_id, request_id) with the INFERENCE_CALL row written by `talkThreadMessage` — same D1 transaction when the fleet tier wins; failover paths emit distinct INFERENCE_CALL rows with the downstream provider but no COMPUTE_SERVE. COMPUTE_SERVE → COIN_MINT to the node operator per COIN § Mint Table. Tokens are sourced from the OSS runtime response (`usage.prompt_tokens` + `usage.completion_tokens`), NOT client-declared — the agent enforces the source-of-truth invariant.

nodes.label: COMPUTE_COORDINATION_TURN
nodes.emitted_by: Emitted by `talk.js:talkThreadMessage` when `scope === 'COMPUTE'` — the governed agent-to-agent coordination channel declared in SERVICES/MAGIC/COMPUTE/ROADMAP.md § Phase 8. Distinguishes ambient coordination turns (status / proposal / decision / blocker) from generic CHAT_TURN so downstream audit can query the COMPUTE principal coordination stream independently. Paired with the existing CHAT_TURN row on (thread_id, turn_id) — CHAT_TURN is the economic event (1 COIN mint per turn per COIN § Mint Table), COMPUTE_COORDINATION_TURN is the observability layer carrying `turn_shape` + `governance_refs` for structured query. Clinical firewall N/A — COMPUTE coord scope carries no PHI by design (content is substrate-operational: attestation keys, operator identity, governance proposals).

nodes.label: EMAIL_SENT
nodes.emitted_by: Emitted by `build-draft-reconcile` (hourly `runtime: worker` TASK Contract — SERVICES/NOTIFIER/CANON § Close the books) on first correlation between a governed email draft (status: draft) and a matching sent message in idrdex@canonic.org Gmail Sent folder (recipient + subject-prefix match). Exactly one row per (draft_path, sent_message_id) — dedup idempotent. Draft's frontmatter flips to `status: sent` atomically with emission. Pairs with `DRAFT_EDITED` LEARNING row in the owning scope's LEARNING.md (edit diff hunks).

nodes.label: EMAIL_DRAFT_UNSENT_FLAGGED
nodes.emitted_by: `build-draft-reconcile` flagging a governed draft with `status: draft` older than 7 days with no correlating sent mail. Transient state — row re-emits on every run until Dexter either sends (→ EMAIL_SENT) or flips frontmatter to `status: abandoned` (→ EMAIL_DRAFT_ABANDONED). Surfaces to build output as WARN.

nodes.label: EMAIL_DRAFT_ABANDONED
nodes.emitted_by: Emitted when Dexter explicitly closes a draft without sending by flipping frontmatter `status: abandoned

nodes.label: EMAIL_DRAFT_ORPHAN
nodes.emitted_by: `build-draft-reconcile` three-way audit — a sent-mail item in Gmail Sent folder older than 24h that does NOT correlate to any governed draft AND is NOT classified as ad-hoc (platform transactional / reply-in-thread / self-directed). Halts the build with the orphan's recipient + subject. Dexter resolves by either creating a backfill governed draft with `status: sent

nodes.label: CALENDAR_EVENT_INSERTED
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/api/calendar/events` — `handleCalendarInsert` in `workers/api.canonic.org/src/calendar.js` (SERVICES/CALENDAR § Operating Surface `write_handlers: [/api/calendar/events]`). One row per user-authored event landing in `calendar_events` D1. The governance-derived events.json from `gen-calendar-events` (TASK CALENDAR-EVENTS) is unaffected — user-authored events live in D1 and are merged by the resolver at read time. Pairs with the `event-detail` view's `update` action (future PUT row will emit CALENDAR_EVENT_UPDATED).

nodes.label: TASK_DISPATCH_REQUESTED
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/api/task/dispatch` — `handleTaskDispatch` in `workers/api.canonic.org/src/task-dispatch.js` (SERVICES/TASK § Operating Surface `write_handlers: [/api/task/dispatch]`). One row per on-demand TASK Contract invocation request. Authed (scoped_admin tier). The handler invokes the target task's GHA workflow via GitHub `workflow_dispatch` REST call OR the worker's `scheduled.generated.js` dispatcher when the TASK runtime is `worker`. The downstream task fire emits its own observability rows (e.g. UPTIME_PROBE, BRANDS_AUDIT) — TASK_DISPATCH_REQUESTED is the audit trace of the trigger, not the run.

nodes.label: BUILD_TRIGGER_REQUESTED
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/api/build/trigger` — `handleBuildTrigger` in `workers/api.canonic.org/src/build.js` (SERVICES/BUILD § Operating Surface `write_handlers: [/api/build/trigger]`, gated by SERVICES/BUILD § Build Control Contract). One row per on-demand build invocation request. Authed (scoped_admin tier; service_owner-only). The handler invokes the `magic-build.yml` GHA workflow via `workflow_dispatch` with the requested gov_root + scope. The build's own session emits `session_started` / `session_completed` / phase rows via the BUILD GUI Phase 1 sidecar — BUILD_TRIGGER_REQUESTED is the audit trace of the trigger, not the build session itself.

nodes.label: BUILD_PHASE_RETRY_REQUESTED
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/api/build/phase/<fn>/retry` — `handleBuildPhaseRetry` in `workers/api.canonic.org/src/build.js` (SERVICES/BUILD § Build Control Contract — `heal` capability gate per AUTH/CANON.md § Capability × Principal Matrix). One row per governor-initiated phase re-run. The handler invokes `magic-build.yml` GHA workflow via `workflow_dispatch` with `phase` and `from_session` inputs so the build orchestrator re-runs the named phase plus its dependent downstream phases. Audit row written before any GH side effect.

nodes.label: BUILD_PHASE_RETRY_DISPATCHED
nodes.emitted_by: Compensating row emitted by `handleBuildPhaseRetry` after `workflow_dispatch` succeeds (HTTP 204). One row per successful retry dispatch; `BUILD_PHASE_RETRY_REQUESTED` records the attempt, this row records the GH acceptance. Pairs on `dispatch_id`.

nodes.label: BUILD_SESSION_CANCEL_REQUESTED
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/api/build/session/<id>/cancel` — `handleBuildSessionCancel` in `workers/api.canonic.org/src/build.js` (SERVICES/BUILD § Build Control Contract — `heal` capability gate). One row per governor-initiated session cancel. Writes a row to `build_control_signals` D1 table with `signal_type='cancel'`; the running `bin/build` orchestrator polls this table at phase boundaries (per `~/.canonic/CANON.md § Debugging Contract`) and SIGTERMs itself when a row matching its `session_id` lands.

nodes.label: BUILD_SESSION_CANCELLED
nodes.emitted_by: Emitted by `bin/build` orchestrator on graceful shutdown after observing a `BUILD_SESSION_CANCEL_REQUESTED` row for its own `session_id`. Pairs on `session_id` with the corresponding cancel-requested row. The build's own `session_completed` event still emits via the SSE sidecar; this row is the audit trace of the cancel-mediated termination.

nodes.label: BUILD_PHASE_CACHE_INVALIDATED
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/api/build/phase/<fn>/cache/invalidate` — `handleBuildPhaseCacheInvalidate` in `workers/api.canonic.org/src/build.js` (SERVICES/BUILD § Build Control Contract — `heal` capability gate). One row per governor-initiated phase cache invalidation. Writes a row to `build_control_signals` D1 with `signal_type='cache_invalidate'`; `bin/build` reads at phase startup and skips the cache short-circuit for any matching `(gov_root, phase_fn)` row whose `cleared_at` is null.

nodes.label: VERIFIER_GRACE_LIFTED
nodes.emitted_by: POST `/api/build/verifier/<name>/grace-lift` — `handleVerifierGraceLift` in `workers/api.canonic.org/src/build.js` (SERVICES/BUILD § Build Control Contract — `heal` capability gate). One row per governor-initiated grace-period lift on a transitional verifier (those marked `transitional: true` with a `sunset_date` in BUILD.md § Production Hardening Gates). Writes a row to `build_control_signals` D1 with `signal_type='grace_lift'`; verify-* gates read at run time and treat their gate as ENFORCE regardless of governance row. `rationale` field required (schema-enforced at boundary, ≥ 16 chars).

nodes.label: SEED_BALANCE
nodes.emitted_by: Migration-only — emitted by governance migrations that seeded `coin_balances.balance` directly without a paired `coin_events` row (legacy: `004-community-seed.sql`, `016-step2ck-abopm-seed.sql`). The compensating `SEED_BALANCE` row brings the ledger reconstruction into agreement with the cache per SERVICES/COIN/COIN.md § Ledger Consistency Contract — required before LEDGER_BALANCE_CONSISTENT gate can flip CLOSED. Bypass-of-Mint-Table: amount sourced from the seed migration declaration, not the COIN § Mint Table. NEVER emitted at runtime — runtime mints flow through `mintForAction()` which writes both sides atomically.

nodes.label: BALANCE_CORRECTION
nodes.emitted_by: Migration-only — emitted to bring `SUM(coin_events.amount)` back into agreement with `coin_balances.balance` when historical drift is detected (e.g. legacy COIN_BACKFILL precision rounding from net-balance seeding the cache while individual lane events compounded to a different sum). The corrective amount is signed (positive when ledger is short, negative when ledger is long). Required by SERVICES/COIN/COIN.md § Ledger Consistency Contract — drift > tolerance is FAIL; the correction event is the auditable governance entry, never a silent `UPDATE coin_balances`. NEVER emitted at runtime — runtime drift signals a writer bug, not a correction opportunity.

nodes.label: TEAM_HANDOFF
nodes.emitted_by: `bin/freshen-issues` — emitted when a GH issue's `team:*` label changes (e.g., `team:compiler` → `team:content`). Per `canonic-canonic/MAGIC/SERVICES/TEAM/CANON.md` § Cross-Team Handoff Protocol the move requires rationale comment + label swap + assignee swap atomically; this row records the protocol-compliant transition and surfaces incomplete handoffs (label-only flips without rationale + assignee) as the dual `TEAM_HANDOFF_INCOMPLETE` warning class. One row per handoff event

nodes.label: AGENT_WORKTREE_ALLOCATED
nodes.emitted_by: `bin/agent-orchestrate` — emitted when a parallel agent worktree is allocated under `~/.canonic/agent-worktrees/<team>/<branch-slug>/`. Pre-dispatch gate `verify-no-concurrent-worktree-conflicts` MUST pass before this row emits; full audit trail of which agent owns which slice (closes the parallel-agent collision class per § Agent Worktree Allocation). Companion `AGENT_WORKTREE_RELEASED` row emits on `agent-orchestrate --release`

nodes.label: MEDRXIV_DOI_MINTED
nodes.emitted_by: Fires when medRxiv returns a DOI for a manuscript submission. Emitter: future automation reading the medRxiv mailbox (correlates with `EMAIL_RECEIVED` matched_task=`MEDRXIV-RECEIPT-*`); for now manual emission via `lib.ledger.emit_governance_event()`. Consumed by the strategic-matrix compiler's `resolve_effective_milestone_date()` to anchor relative milestone dates on Pipeline-1 sibling DOIs (per `hadleylab-canonic/CANON.md § strategic_interlock` `milestone_anchor_event`). One row per (scope, version) DOI mint

nodes.label: MANUSCRIPT_DRAFT_FROZEN
nodes.emitted_by: Fires when a manuscript's `draft-v<N>.md` is declared frozen for preprint submission (content_hash captured at the freeze; downstream MEDRXIV_DOI_MINTED row pairs on scope). Emitter: manual via `lib.ledger.emit_governance_event()` (operator-staged at draft-freeze ceremony). Consumed by the strategic-matrix compiler's `resolve_effective_milestone_date()` to compute journal-cycle milestones as `frozen_ts + offset_days` (per CANON § strategic_interlock `milestone_anchor_event`). One row per (scope, draft_version) freeze

nodes.label: STEALTH_PUBLISH_PROPOSED
nodes.emitted_by: `bin/propose-paper-publish` only — fired exactly once per upstream `MEDRXIV_DOI_MINTED` event whose corresponding `MANUSCRIPTS/<scope>/MANUSCRIPT.md` still has `stealth: true` AND `stealth_cleared_event: null`. Propose-mode side effect: writes `hadleylab-canonic/PAPERS/<slug>.draft.md` byte-identical to the verified `canonical_hash_source` artifact (the `.draft.md` suffix is governed staging convention — NOT live; the live `PAPERS/<slug>.md` is created later by the operator-only `bin/promote-paper-publish` after review). Hard-fails the gate (exit 1) on any of: `canonical_hash_source` missing, re-hash drift vs declared `canonical_hash`, `PAPERS/<slug>.md` already exists (prior promotion ran), or `.draft.md` already exists with content not matching the canonical source (hand-edit detected). Idempotent: re-running with no new DOI events emits zero rows; re-running after a STEALTH_PUBLISH_PROPOSED already exists for the same (scope, doi) skips. NEVER mutates the MANUSCRIPT.md `stealth*` fields and NEVER emits STEALTH_CLEARED — that is the operator-mediated promote step

nodes.label: INSTRUMENT_EXECUTED
nodes.emitted_by: `bin/instrument-execute` — emitted when a Required-Instruments row in `SERVICES/LEGAL/CANON.md § Required Instruments` flips to `status: executed` after deposit of the signed PDF at `LEGAL/INSTRUMENTS/<id>/executed.pdf`. Move 4 of the Restricted-Entities Closure (cease-and-desist compliance). Pairs with one or more downstream `RESTRICTION_LIFTED_VIA_INSTRUMENT` rows. Hard-fails when the PDF is missing, the SHA-256 mismatches, or the instrument_id is not declared in CANON

nodes.label: RESTRICTION_LIFTED_VIA_INSTRUMENT
nodes.emitted_by: `bin/instrument-execute` — emitted when one or more `SERVICES/LEGAL/CANON.md § Restricted Entities` rows are removed because their `until_instrument` reached `status: executed`. One row per instrument-keyed lift event; payload enumerates the lifted (entity, restriction_class) pairs. Move 4 of the Restricted-Entities Closure

nodes.label: PRIVATE_ACT_RELEASED
nodes.emitted_by: Emitted when a `SERVICES/LEGAL/CANON.md § Private-Act Holds` row is released because its `release_on_instrument` is `executed` AND any required counterparty-specific independent review (`INDEPENDENT_REVIEW_COMPLETE` row) has landed. Move 4 of the Restricted-Entities Closure. The held artifact's hash is preserved; release is the pre-condition for renewed prosecution / amendment / submission

nodes.label: INDEPENDENT_REVIEW_COMPLETE
nodes.emitted_by: Emitted when counterparty-specific independent review of a held artifact (e.g., Dr. Nimrod's IP counsel review under CAOH-NOTICE-2026-04-27 II(c); her authorship review under II(d)) is recorded. Pairs with downstream `PRIVATE_ACT_RELEASED` row when the review unblocks a held artifact

nodes.label: RESTRICTED_ENTITY_DECLARED
nodes.emitted_by: Emitted by `bin/gen-restricted-entities` (or operator-staged ceremony for new C&D notices) on first observation of a new row in `SERVICES/LEGAL/CANON.md § Restricted Entities`. One row per (entity, restriction_class) pair on first declaration. Idempotent re-emits suppressed via per-pair marker

nodes.label: PUBLIC_CLAIM_APPROVED
nodes.emitted_by: Counsel-routing-mediated ratification of a public claim that would otherwise FAIL `verify-no-public-restricted-reference` or a sibling restricted-entity gate. Emitted by an operator-staged ceremony only (no automatic emitter). Used sparingly when an exception is genuinely on counsel record

nodes.label: SESSION_OPENED
nodes.emitted_by: `bin/session-open S<N> --intent "<framing>"` — emitted exactly once per governance session when the operator opens a new work window. Per `canonic-canonic/MAGIC/SERVICES/SESSION/CANON.md` § Lifecycle States + § Context-Binding Contract. Flips the `active_session` ANCHOR to `S<N>` and records the operator's declared intent (≤500 chars), prior-session inheritance list, and a content-hash snapshot of `~/.claude/projects/-Users-iDrDex-CANONIC/memory/MEMORY.md` at open time. Emitter is `bin/session-open` exclusively via `lib.ledger.emit_session_event`. Bootstrap event for S39 carries `inherits_open_items_from: [S38]` even though S38 has no SESSION_CLOSED row in LEDGER (predates the primitive). Retroactive S2-S38 emissions carry `opened_date_inferred: true` flag

nodes.label: SESSION_CLOSED
nodes.emitted_by: `bin/session-close S<N>` — emitted exactly once per governance session on close. Gated by `verify-session-no-orphan-issues` (every issue raised in the session window MUST be CLOSED or CARRIED_FORWARD). Cross-references the conversation transcripts that produced the session by querying the LEDGER for SCAN_TRANSCRIPT events whose `ts` falls in `[SESSION_OPENED.ts, now()]` and attaching their SHAs to the close payload (auto-discovered, operator never types transcript hashes manually). Captures memory-snapshot hash at close to enable diff against open. Pairs with the downstream HANDOFF_WRITTEN row

nodes.label: HANDOFF_WRITTEN
nodes.emitted_by: `bin/session-handoff S<N>` — emitted when the schema-conformant handoff materialises at `hadleylab-canonic/SERVICES/SESSION/HANDOFFS/S<N>/HANDOFF.md` per `canonic-canonic/MAGIC/SERVICES/SESSION/CANON.md` § Handoff Schema. Pairs with the upstream SESSION_CLOSED row (handoff is materialised inside the close ceremony). The `schema_version` field on the payload is bumped if the required sections list changes shape — old handoffs remain valid against their own schema_version

nodes.label: ISSUE_CARRIED_FORWARD
nodes.emitted_by: `bin/session-reconcile-gh` (build-time poll) + GH webhook (live `api.canonic.org/gh/webhook` handler) — emitted when an issue raised in S<N> cannot resolve in S<N> and is explicitly carried to S<N+1> per `canonic-canonic/MAGIC/SERVICES/SESSION/CANON.md` § Carry-Forward (three-layer: GH label `gov-session-carry:S<N>→S<N+1>` + LEDGER row + handoff frontmatter `carried_forward: [#numbers]`). Idempotency-keyed via `lib.ledger.emit_session_event` so webhook + reconcile dedupe at append time

nodes.label: WORKFLOW_RUN_FAILED
nodes.emitted_by: GH webhook `workflow_run.completed` handler in `api.canonic.org/gh/webhook` — emitted only when the workflow run conclusion is FAILED (green runs are deferred to v2 per ROADMAP § Later). Carries the `gov_session` resolved from the SESSION ANCHOR at emission time so failed CI runs are session-attributable for trend analysis. Open items the session must address before close; verify-session-no-orphan-issues HALTs if a WORKFLOW_RUN_FAILED in the session window has no follow-up issue

nodes.label: PLAN_ABSORBED
nodes.emitted_by: `~/.canonic/bin/promote-plans` — emitted once per scratch plan file under the SCAN_ROOTS declared in `canonic-canonic/MAGIC/SERVICES/SESSION/CANON.md § Plan Absorption Contract`. Idempotent on `plan_sha256` — re-running on the same plan content is a no-op. Classification is one of {GH_ISSUE, ROADMAP_ROW, LEARNING_ROW, DELETE} per the contract's classification rules; `target_uri` resolves to a typed pointer at the absorption sink (or `pending://` if the human-routing step hasn't run yet — gate only requires the row exists). Pairs with `PLAN_ABSORB_OVERRIDE` for stale plans that ship past the 7-day freshness threshold

nodes.label: PLAN_ABSORB_OVERRIDE
nodes.emitted_by: `~/.canonic/bin/verify-plans-absorbed --allow-stale --reason "<why>"` — emitted exactly once per (plan_sha256, override-invocation) when the operator escapes the freshness gate during session-close. Same shape as `--override-freeze`. Does NOT replace the `PLAN_ABSORBED` requirement — the override is recorded so trend analysis can detect operators who repeatedly defer plan absorption

nodes.label: {TASK_NAME}
nodes.worker: (runtime: worker only) path to CF Worker source file
nodes.handler: (runtime: worker only) named export in the worker file
